en.extract:
The fluffy-backed tit-babbler (Macronus ptilosus) (Thai: นกกินแมลงหลังฟู) is a species of bird in the family Timaliidae. It is found in Brunei, Indonesia, Malaysia, and Thailand. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical swampland. It is threatened by habitat loss.
There are two subspecies recognized by the International Ornithological Congress:

M. p. ptilosus (Jardine & Selby, 1835) - Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, Batu Islands
M. p. trichorrhos (Temminck, 1836) - Bangka Belitung Islands and Borneo


References


Further reading
Collar, N. J. & Robson, C. 2007. Family Timaliidae (Babblers)  pp. 70–291 in; del Hoyo, J., Elliott, A. & Christie, D.A. eds. Handbook of the Birds of the World, Vol. 12. Picathartes to Tits and Chickadees. Lynx Edicions, Barcelona.

The '''fluffy-backed tit-babbler''' ('''''Macronus ptilosus''''') (Thai: นกกินแมลงหลังฟู)<ref name="thai">{{Cite web|url=https://www.thainationalparks.com/species/fluffy-backed-tit-babbler|title = Macronus ptilosus, Fluffy-backed tit-babbler}}</ref> is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Timaliidae]]. It is found in [[Brunei]], [[Indonesia]], [[Malaysia]], and [[Thailand]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical [[swampland]]. It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].<ref name="thai"/>

There are two subspecies recognized by the [[International Ornithologists' Union|International Ornithological Congress]]:<ref>{{Cite web |title=Babblers, scimitar babblers, ground babblers, Alcippe fulvettas – IOC World Bird List |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/babblers/ |access-date=2023-01-23 |language=en-US}}</ref>

* ''M. p. ptilosus'' ([[Sir William Jardine, 7th Baronet|Jardine]] & [[Prideaux John Selby|Selby]], 1835) - [[Malay Peninsula]], [[Sumatra]], [[Batu Islands]]
* ''M. p. trichorrhos'' ([[Coenraad Jacob Temminck|Temminck]], 1836) - [[Bangka Belitung Islands]] and [[Borneo]]
